title: ranunculus orbicans
intro: Ranunculus orbicans, also known as the pale buttercup, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Ranunculus orbicans include its potential to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, as well as its use as a digestive aid and to promote sweating. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of tinctures, infusions, or ointments, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity and allergic reactions.
